reforms.description: Australia is focusing on reducing regulatory, administrative, and financial barriers for young high potential firms to support their growth and innovation capacity.
reforms.component_name: Support for Young High Potential Firms
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By facilitating the growth of innovative firms, the reform is expected to enhance productivity and contribute positively to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The focus on reducing barriers for young high potential firms was highlighted as a key policy insight.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it targets a specific segment of the economy that can drive innovation and job creation, although it is not a comprehensive overhaul of the innovation system.
reforms.source_quote: Recent efforts to reduce regulatory, administrative and financial barriers for young high potential firms should continue.

reforms.description: The Australian government has reached an intergovernmental agreement on automatic mutual recognition of occupational licenses across jurisdictions, which will simplify the licensing process for workers and reduce administrative costs.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By reducing barriers to labor mobility and administrative costs, this reform is expected to enhance productivity and support the growth of innovative firms.
reforms.status_evidence: The federal government has passed enabling legislation for the reform.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant overhaul of the occupational licensing system, which has historically been a barrier to labor mobility and business dynamism, thus having a broad impact on the economy.
reforms.source_quote: The Commonwealth, State and Territory governments have now reached an intergovernmental agreement on automatic mutual recognition and begun to implement the scheme.

reforms.description: The Australian government announced new training supports as part of the 2021-22 Federal Budget, including an extension of an apprentice wage subsidy and additional affordable training courses for young people.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing skills and training for the unemployed can improve employability and productivity, contributing positively to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government announced new training supports as part of the budget.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of new training supports represents a significant step towards improving workforce skills, which is crucial for addressing unemployment and enhancing economic productivity.
reforms.source_quote: As part of the 2021-22 Federal Budget, the government announced some new training supports, including an extension of an apprentice wage subsidy.

reforms.description: The Australian government is adopting a New Employment Services Model to replace the existing Jobactive programme, emphasizing digital services for job matching and training.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing job matching and training services, the new model aims to improve employment outcomes and productivity, fostering economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government is adopting a New Employment Services Model that will replace the existing Jobactive programme from 2022.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant overhaul of employment services, aiming to modernize the approach to job matching and training, which is critical for addressing unemployment and enhancing workforce skills.
reforms.source_quote: The government is adopting a New Employment Services Model that will replace the existing Jobactive programme from 2022.

reforms.description: The Australian Government announced changes to the R&D Tax Incentive, increasing the expenditure threshold from AUD100 million to AUD150 million and adjusting offset rates based on company tax rates and R&D intensity.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing the R&D Tax Incentive, the reform aims to stimulate private sector investment in R&D, thereby fostering innovation and productivity growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The changes to the R&D Tax Incentive were announced in the 2020-21 Budget and took effect on 1 July 2021.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant overhaul of the R&D Tax Incentive, which is crucial for encouraging private sector R&D investment and enhancing Australia's innovation landscape.
reforms.source_quote: In the 2020-21 Budget the Government announced that, as of 1 July 2021, the R&D expenditure threshold will increase from AUD100 million to AUD150 million.

reforms.description: The Government introduced a patent box to incentivize the development and application of medical and biotechnology innovations in Australia, taxing profits from these innovations at a concessional rate.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The patent box aims to attract and retain innovative firms in the medical and biotechnology sectors, promoting investment and job creation in high-value industries.
reforms.status_evidence: In the 2021-22 Budget the Government announced a patent box to encourage companies to develop and apply their medical and biotechnology innovations in Australia.
reforms.importance_rationale: While the patent box is a significant incentive for specific sectors, it is a targeted reform rather than a comprehensive overhaul of the innovation system.
reforms.source_quote: In the 2021-22 Budget the Government announced a patent box to encourage companies to develop and apply their medical and biotechnology innovations in Australia.

reforms.description: The Government announced the AUD800 million Digital Business Plan to enhance digital government services, including the development of a Digital Identity system and the Modernising Business Registers program.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By improving digital infrastructure and services, the reform aims to enhance efficiency in public service delivery and support innovation in the private sector.
reforms.status_evidence: Key initiatives of the Digital Business Plan were announced and funding allocated in September 2020.
reforms.importance_rationale: The Digital Business Plan represents a significant investment in digital infrastructure, which is essential for modernizing public services and fostering innovation.
reforms.source_quote: In September 2020, the Government announced the AUD800m Digital Business Plan.

reforms.description: The Australian government has released the Technology Investment Roadmap, which identifies key areas for technological development to reduce emissions, guiding AUD20 billion of government investment over the next decade.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The investment in clean technologies is expected to enhance productivity and innovation in carbon-intensive sectors, contributing to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The roadmap has been released and outlines government investment plans.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in government policy towards targeted investment in clean technologies, which is crucial for meeting climate goals and fostering innovation.
reforms.source_quote: In 2020, the government released the Technology Investment Roadmap that identified five key areas where technological developments could reduce emissions in Australia’s most carbon-intensive sectors.

reforms.description: The Australian Renewable Energy Agency provides grants for research, development, demonstration, deployment, and early-stage commercialisation of renewable technology, supporting the transition to clean energy.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Funding for renewable energy projects is expected to stimulate innovation and create jobs in the clean energy sector, contributing to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The Australian Renewable Energy Agency continues to provide grants for various renewable energy initiatives.
reforms.importance_rationale: This funding mechanism is significant for promoting renewable energy technologies but is part of a broader strategy rather than a standalone major reform.
reforms.source_quote: The Australian Renewable Energy Agency provides grants for research, development, demonstration, deployment and early-stage commercialisation of renewables technology.

reforms.description: The federal government announced co-investment with the private sector from the AUD71.9 million Future Fuels Fund to address charging blackspots and demonstrate hydrogen refuelling infrastructure, aiming to enhance electric vehicle adoption.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving charging infrastructure is essential for increasing electric vehicle adoption, which can lead to reduced emissions and foster innovation in the automotive sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The federal government recently announced co-investment with the private sector.
reforms.importance_rationale: This investment is significant as it targets a critical barrier to electric vehicle adoption, which is essential for achieving broader environmental goals.
reforms.source_quote: The federal government recently announced co-investment with the private sector from the AUD71.9 million Future Fuels Fund to address 'charging blackspots' and to demonstrate hydrogen refuelling infrastructure.

reforms.description: The Australian government has introduced a special restructuring process and simplified liquidation process for incorporated SMEs, aimed at reducing costs and encouraging earlier action to preserve economic value.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By facilitating easier restructuring, the reform helps viable firms to survive and adapt, thereby supporting overall economic productivity and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The new restructuring and liquidation processes have been established and are in effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant overhaul of the insolvency framework for SMEs, which is crucial for maintaining entrepreneurship and innovation in the economy.
reforms.source_quote: The government has created a special restructuring process and simplified liquidation process for incorporated SMEs.

reforms.description: The Australian government established the Australian Business Securitisation Fund in 2019 to develop the market for securitised SME loans by investing in securitised assets and warehouses backed by loans issued by non-bank lenders and smaller banks.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By facilitating access to finance for smaller banks and non-bank lenders, the fund aims to lower funding costs and improve lending conditions for SMEs, thereby supporting innovation and growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The first securitisation involving the Fund was launched in early 2021.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of the fund represents a significant step towards improving the financing landscape for SMEs, which is crucial for fostering innovation and entrepreneurship.
reforms.source_quote: The government established the Australian Business Securitisation Fund in 2019 to develop the market by investing in securitised assets and in warehouses backed by loans issued by non-bank lenders and smaller banks.

reforms.description: The Australian government is considering reforms to simplify the Personal Property Securities Act to improve its effectiveness for innovative firms with intangible assets, which could enhance access to credit.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Simplifying the Act would reduce compliance costs and improve lenders' confidence in using intangible assets as collateral, thereby facilitating access to finance for innovative firms.
reforms.status_evidence: The government is considering its response to the 2015 statutory review.
reforms.importance_rationale: The proposed reforms aim to address significant barriers faced by innovative firms in accessing finance, which is critical for their growth and development.
reforms.source_quote: The independent statutory review of the Act highlighted the need to simplify the Act to generate more predictable outcomes, which would give lenders greater confidence.

reforms.description: The Australian government announced two new visas for high-skilled workers to attract talent in innovation sectors, alongside plans to modernise the tax treatment of employee share schemes.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Attracting high-skilled workers enhances the talent pool, which is crucial for driving innovation and productivity in the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The government announced two new visas for high-skilled workers.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative is significant as it aims to enhance the skilled workforce, which is essential for fostering innovation and economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: In May 2021 the government announced two new visas for high-skilled workers and plans to modernise the tax treatment of employee share schemes.

reforms.description: The Australian government is considering adjustments to public equity regulations to facilitate easier scaling back of investments by company founders, which could enhance access to capital for innovative startups.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Easing regulations can improve access to capital for startups, fostering innovation and growth in the entrepreneurial sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The Committee has recommended adjusting public equity regulations to make it easier for company founders to scale back their investment.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it addresses barriers to capital access for startups, which is crucial for their growth and innovation potential.
reforms.source_quote: The Committee has recommended adjusting public equity regulations to make it easier for company founders to scale back their investment.

reforms.description: The Australian government created two industry-specific co-investment funds as part of the National Innovation and Science Agenda, including a AUD500 million Biomedical Translation Fund and a AUD240 million CSIRO Innovation Fund, aimed at enhancing venture capital for innovative sectors.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: These funds are designed to stimulate investment in innovative sectors, thereby enhancing the overall venture capital ecosystem and supporting the growth of startups.
reforms.status_evidence: The funds were created as part of the National Innovation and Science Agenda.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of these co-investment funds represents a significant structural change in the venture capital landscape, aimed at fostering innovation and supporting the growth of the startup ecosystem.
reforms.source_quote: The government has been building the venture capital eco-system. As part of the National Innovation and Science Agenda it created two industry-specific, government-led co-investment funds.

reforms.description: The Australian government launched the AUD540 million Australian Business Growth Fund to ease financing constraints for SMEs through equity investment, with a focus on established firms rather than high-growth startups.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By providing equity investment to SMEs, the fund aims to alleviate financing constraints, thereby supporting their growth and innovation potential.
reforms.status_evidence: The government launched the AUD540 million Australian Business Growth Fund.
reforms.importance_rationale: This fund represents a significant but narrower initiative aimed at supporting SMEs, which are crucial for innovation and economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: In 2020 the government launched the AUD540 million Australian Business Growth Fund with AUD100 million of public funds and the remainder from six banks.

reforms.description: The Australian government announced an investment of AUD209.7 million to establish the Australian Climate Service, aimed at enhancing the country's capacity to manage and adapt to natural disasters.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This investment is expected to improve disaster management capabilities, thereby enhancing resilience and reducing economic losses from climate-related events.
reforms.status_evidence: The investment was included in the 2021-22 Budget.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant investment in infrastructure aimed at improving climate resilience, which is crucial for mitigating the economic impacts of natural disasters.
reforms.source_quote: The government announced in the 2021-22 Budget investment of AUD209.7 million to establish the Australian Climate Service.

reforms.description: The Australian government announced AUD615.5 million for the Preparing Australia Program to provide grants for projects that support public and private disaster risk reduction and resilience.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This funding is intended to enhance resilience against disasters, which can lead to reduced economic losses and improved recovery times.
reforms.status_evidence: The funding was announced as part of the 2021-22 Budget.
reforms.importance_rationale: This program represents a significant allocation of resources aimed at enhancing disaster resilience, which is critical for long-term economic stability.
reforms.source_quote: The government has also announced AUD615.5 million for the Preparing Australia Program to provide grants for projects that support public and private disaster risk reduction and resilience.

reforms.description: Australia aims to develop a coherent national strategy for achieving emission reduction objectives, leveraging its natural endowments and human capital for innovation in carbon abatement technologies.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By focusing on innovation in carbon abatement technologies, the strategy aims to create new economic opportunities while addressing climate change.
reforms.status_evidence: A coherent and coordinated national strategy that defines clear goals and corresponding policy settings will be critical.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it aims to align innovation efforts with national climate goals, potentially transforming the economy towards sustainability.
reforms.source_quote: A coherent and coordinated national strategy that defines clear goals and corresponding policy settings for achieving the government’s emission reduction objectives will be critical.
